workflow.name: Research — Market / Domain / Technical
workflow.critical: Web search is REQUIRED — if unavailable, HALT immediately
Use parallel web searches for efficiency
ALL claims must have citations with URLs — no unsourced assertions
Multi-source validation for critical claims (market size, growth rates, competitive data)
Add confidence levels (High/Medium/Low) on uncertain data
Write findings to document IMMEDIATELY as discovered — do NOT batch at the end

step.action: Ask user: 1. What topic do you want to research? 2. What type? market (customer/competition) / domain (industry/regulatory) / technical (architecture/tools) 3. What specific goals or questions to answer? 4. Any geographic or segment constraints?
Check for existing research in _flow_output/planning-artifacts/research/ on this topic

check.action: Load it, summarize what's already covered, identify gaps to fill
check.ask: Continuer cette recherche existante ou en demarrer une nouvelle?

step.action: Confirm scope with user: topic, type, goals, constraints
Write research header: topic, type, goals, methodology, date

step.goal: Core research — parallel web searches by type
step.critical: Launch multiple web searches in parallel for efficiency

check.action: Research in parallel: - Market size + growth projections (TAM/SAM/SOM if applicable) - Customer segments + behavior patterns - Key trends and market dynamics - Pricing models and revenue patterns
For each data point: source URL, date, confidence level
Write "Market Overview" section to document immediately
Research in parallel: - Industry structure and key players - Domain-specific terminology and concepts - Current state and evolution trajectory - Best practices and established patterns
Write "Domain Analysis" section to document immediately
Research in parallel: - Technology landscape and alternatives - Performance benchmarks and comparisons - Community size, maturity, maintenance status - Integration patterns and compatibility
Write "Technical Evaluation" section to document immediately
Identify top 5-10 competitors via web search
For each: positioning, pricing, strengths/weaknesses, market share (if available)
Competitive matrix: feature comparison table
Identify market gaps and differentiation opportunities
Map industry ecosystem: suppliers, buyers, substitutes, new entrants
Identify leading organizations and their approaches
Map domain-specific tools, platforms, standards
Compare top 3-5 technology options side-by-side
Decision matrix: performance, DX, community, cost, scalability
Document migration paths and lock-in risks

step.action: Write competitive/landscape section to document immediately

step.goal: Deep dive — specific to research type

check.action: Customer pain points: top 5 problems with evidence
Purchase decision factors: what drives buying decisions
Customer journey mapping: awareness → consideration → decision
Regulatory landscape: current and upcoming regulations
Compliance requirements specific to the domain
Industry standards and certifications
Architecture patterns: recommended approaches with trade-offs
Implementation considerations: common pitfalls, gotchas, versioning
Security and performance implications

step.action: Write deep dive section to document
Compile key findings across all sections

Write synthesis: ## Key Findings - Top 5 critical insights (with source references)
## Strategic Recommendations - 3-5 actionable recommendations ranked by impact - For each: rationale, evidence, implementation hint
## Risks and Uncertainties - Data gaps identified (what we couldn't find) - Low-confidence claims that need validation - Market/domain risks to monitor
## Next Steps - Recommended follow-up research - Decisions this research enables - Connection to project planning (/brainstorm or /story)
step.output: Recherche terminee! Fichier: _flow_output/planning-artifacts/research/{type}-{topic}-{date}.md {{findings_count}} insights cles, {{recommendations_count}} recommandations. Prochaine etape: /story pour creer des stories basees sur ces insights, ou /brainstorm pour explorer les opportunites.
